statement: Iceland is a member of the Arctic Council and chairs the Council of Europe (distinct from the EU). The EU has sought observer status in the Arctic Council since 2008 but has been repeatedly blocked, most recently by Russia. Three EU member states (Denmark/Greenland, Finland, Sweden) are Arctic Council members. The EU's Arctic policy, updated in 2021, emphasises climate change, sustainable development, and international cooperation. EU membership would not diminish Iceland's own Arctic Council seat but would add an EU coordination layer to Iceland's Arctic policy positions.
source_name: European Commission — EU Arctic Policy, Joint Communication JOIN(2021) 27
caveats: Arctic geopolitics have shifted significantly since Russia's suspension from Arctic Council cooperation in 2022. Iceland's strategic position in the North Atlantic (GIUK gap) has gained renewed military significance. EU membership could strengthen or complicate Iceland's independent Arctic voice depending on alignment of interests.

related_entries.statement: Sjálfstæðisflokkurinn (Independence Party) introduced a bill in Althingi proposing an 18-month cooling-off period that would prevent senior officials and ministerial advisors involved in negotiations with international organisations from accepting positions with those organisations after leaving office. The bill was introduced in the context of potential EU accession negotiations, framed as a safeguard against conflicts of interest. Similar 'revolving door' regulations exist at the EU institutional level: EU Commissioners face an 18-month cooling-off period (extended to 24 months for the Commission President), and the European Ombudsman has called for stricter enforcement. Several EU member states, including France and Germany, have analogous rules for senior civil servants.

citations.report_title: 25. þáttur Silfursins: Stefnt að kosningu um ESB í sumar

claims.claim_text: Hagsmunamat ESB er núna þannig að sambandið lítur á stóru myndina og vill að líkt þenkjandi ríki standi saman, til dæmis í tollastríði.

citations.report_title: Engin fordæmi fyrir leið Íslands í átt að ESB

claims.claim_text: Margar breytingar hafa átt sér stað í alþjóðamálum undanfarin tíu ár sem hafa áhrif á samningaviðræður.
Samningsstaða Íslands gagnvart ESB er sterkari nú en hún var fyrir tíu árum.

citations.report_title: ESB og sjávarútvegurinn: Hver á að ráða hafinu við Ísland?

claims.claim_text: Vaxandi alþjóðlegur þrýstingur frá Evrópu er um aukna vernd hvala og friðun hafsvæða.
